key_insight: Latent Drift forecasts slow disease progression by modeling anatomical change in a compressed latent space, helping the model focus on subtle drift instead of reproducing static baseline MRI anatomy.
clinical_relevance: This could improve earlier prediction of neurodegenerative progression and support better-targeted interventions and more informative clinical trial design.
method: Learns progression in a semantic latent representation rather than full-resolution images
Uses Finite Scalar Quantization to reduce nuisance noise and preserve consistent structural drift
Addresses two failure modes: identity collapse and the continuous interpolation trap
evidence_signal: In longitudinal 3D brain MRI experiments, Latent Drift outperformed diffusion and autoregressive transformer baselines on generative fidelity and clinically relevant evaluation metrics.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and scale of longitudinal MRI data, and latent forecasts still need careful validation before being used in real-world clinical workflows.
paper_title: Progression as Latent Drift: Generative Forecasting of Slow-Evolving Pathologies
